[0,1].name: Missouri River
[0,1].desc: Follow the current north. Safe, steady progress on the river highway.

[0,1].name: Overland Trail
[0,1].desc: Cut across Missouri prairie. Faster march but punishing heat and rough terrain.

[1,2].name: Missouri River
[1,2].desc: The proven river route continues northward through Otoe lands.

[1,2].name: Prairie Shortcut
[1,2].desc: Gamble through open plains. Swift if luck holds — dangerous if it doesn't.

[2,3].name: River Route
[2,3].desc: Steady Missouri progress. Sioux bands may demand toll.

[2,3].name: Plains Crossing
[2,3].desc: March through the tall-grass sea. Remote but Teton Sioux territory is everywhere.

[3,4].name: North Fork
[3,4].desc: A longer but sheltered channel — fewer confrontations, more fuel for fires.

[3,4].name: Main Channel
[3,4].desc: The direct Missouri route north toward Mandan country.

[4,5].name: Missouri Headwaters
[4,5].desc: Follow the river to its mountain origin. Sublime but demanding.

[4,5].name: Overland March
[4,5].desc: Strike cross-country toward the Yellowstone. Hard ground, harder weather.

[5,6].name: River Trail
[5,6].desc: The upper Missouri narrows between canyon walls. Spectacular — and perilous.

[5,6].name: Mountain Bypass
[5,6].desc: A rugged shortcut through the Rocky foothills. Saves days, costs health.

[6,7].name: Shoshone Pass
[6,7].desc: The Shoshone have crossed this pass for generations. Seek their guidance.

[6,7].name: Bitterroot Valley
[6,7].desc: Grueling forest canyons. Beautiful, brutal. The hardest march of the expedition.

[7,8].name: Nez Perce Trail
[7,8].desc: The Nez Perce know every pass. With their guidance, the crossing is manageable.

[7,8].name: Ridge Route
[7,8].desc: A high spine traverse with no cover. Ice, altitude, exposure.

[8,9].name: Columbia River
[8,9].desc: Ride the great Columbia to tidewater. The river does the work.

[8,9].name: Coastal Trail
[8,9].desc: Dense Pacific rainforest — wet, cold, exhausting. But you smell the ocean.
